headline: CONFIRMATION. No new acute April 24-29 net evidence beyond April 17 Frontier Safety Framework v3 (Tracked Capability Levels, harmful-manipulation CCL, internal-deployment safety case requirements) — already absorbed in April 28 confirmation. Boston Dynamics Atlas factory deployment ramp with Hyundai/Google DeepMind fleet allocations (2028 deployment target) is sector-context, not entity-acute. AI labor sector alert (92K+ tech layoffs) is contextual; DeepMind/Google parent has not announced direct AI-displacement-rationale layoffs in window. Composite 65.0 (Established, near 80-band boundary) holds.

evidence.source: DeepMind Frontier Safety Framework v3 April 17 2026
evidence.finding: FSF v3 published with Tracked Capability Levels, harmful-manipulation CCL, internal-deployment safety case requirements. Pre-EU AI Act enforcement (96 days). Lacks specified independent external oversight (limits ACC upgrade).
Sector context: 92K+ tech sector layoffs in 2026 partly enabled by AI automation. DeepMind/Google parent not in window-specific layoff list.

negativeSignals: Sector-context AI labor cascade pressure on parent (Alphabet); not entity-acute for DeepMind specifically.
positiveSignals: FSF v3 pre-enforcement positioning ahead of EU AI Act; explicit internal-deployment safety case requirements.
